title: agrostis chamissonis
intro: Agrostis chamissonis, also known as Chamisso's bent or beach bent grass, is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. This plant is native to the coastal regions of the Pacific Northwest and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The roots of Agrostis chamissonis are said to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as wounds, fever, and digestive problems. Agrostis chamissonis can be prepared as a tea, decoction, or infusion for its medicinal benefits, and it has been used to treat a range of health issues, from skin conditions to respiratory problems.
